home.title: Steak Festival
home.subtitle: 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 August
home.aboutText: Chianina beef, cooked rare following the ancient Tuscan tradition, is the star of one of the most beloved food festivals in the Valdichiana. Every year, thousands of visitors gather to celebrate this unique gastronomic ritual.
home.grillText: Over 14 meters long — the largest functioning grill in Italy. Every evening, our expert grillers cook hundreds of steaks to perfection, honoring tradition.
home.locationText: Giardini del Parterre, in the heart of Cortona. A unique atmosphere within the ancient Etruscan walls of the city, every summer alive with the magic of the sagra.
home.historyText: Founded in the 1960s, the Sagra della Bistecca di Cortona is one of Tuscany's longest-running and most beloved food festivals.
home.ctaDesc: Book your spot at Tuscany's most beloved festival

tickets.title: Tickets
tickets.subtitle: Buy online or at the gate
tickets.onlineNote:
Tickets purchased online entitle the holder to receive the included items once during the entire event.
This means that, regardless of how many days the event runs, the ticket contents — such as steak, hamburger, or other benefits — may be redeemed or used only once, on whichever day you choose during the Sagra della Bistecca 2026.
tickets.onlineText: Purchase your tickets conveniently online through Tailor Tickets.
tickets.gateText: Tickets will also be available directly at the ticket desk on the evening of the event.

ourStory.title: Our Story
ourStory.subtitle: Over 60 years of tradition
ourStory.p1: The Sagra della Bistecca di Cortona was born in the 1960s as a moment of celebration and community for the people of Cortona. What started as a simple communal dinner has grown over the decades into one of Tuscany's most renowned food festivals.
ourStory.p2: The undisputed star is always the same: Chianina beef, a prized cut from the native cattle breed of the Valdichiana, cooked rare following the traditional recipe. A simple preparation that respects the raw ingredient and enhances the authentic flavor of the meat.
ourStory.p3: The grill, over 14 meters long, is the festival's symbol — the largest functioning grill in Italy. Every evening of the event, the master grillers cook hundreds of steaks with the same care and passion as always.
ourStory.p4: After more than 60 editions, the Sagra della Bistecca remains an unmissable event for locals and visitors from around the world, set in the magnificent Giardini del Parterre, with the Valle di Cortona as a backdrop.

contacts.title: Contacts
contacts.subtitle: We are here to help

form.name: Full Name
form.success: Request sent! We'll get back to you as soon as possible.
form.error: Error sending. Please try again or contact us directly.

contacts.groupsNote: For couples or groups of fewer than 10 people, no reservation is needed: there are plenty of tables where you can sit and enjoy your meal.

items.q: Do I need to book a table?
items.a: For couples or small groups (fewer than 10 people), no reservation is needed. There are plenty of tables available every evening. Reservations are required only for groups of 10 or more — in that case, contact us via the Contacts page.

items.q: Can I buy tickets on the evening of the event?
items.a: Yes, tickets are available directly at the ticket desk on the evening of the event, subject to availability. To avoid queuing, we recommend purchasing online in advance.

items.q: Is an online ticket valid for all days of the festival?
items.a: An online ticket entitles you to receive the included items once during the entire event, on whichever day you choose between 11 and 15 August 2026.

items.q: What does the steak menu include?
items.a: The steak menu (€39) includes: Chianina steak 750/800g cooked rare, a choice of side dish (cannellini beans or tomatoes), 1 liter of water, and bread.

items.q: Is there a menu for children?
items.a: Yes! The children's menu (€18) includes: Chianina burger 140g, french fries, a soft drink or water, and cover charge.

items.q: Is the steak always cooked rare?
items.a: Yes. The Chianina steak is cooked rare following the ancient Tuscan tradition, seasoned with coarse salt and olive oil. If you prefer it more cooked or well done, just ask!

items.q: Where does the festival take place?
items.a: The Sagra della Bistecca takes place at the Giardini del Parterre, in the heart of Cortona (AR), with the stunning Valle di Cortona as a backdrop.

items.q: How do I get to Cortona?
items.a: By car: Motorway A1, exit Valdichiana, then follow signs to Cortona. By train: Camucia-Cortona station (Rome–Florence line), then taxi or shuttle bus. By plane: Florence airport (1h) or Rome Fiumicino (2h).
Yes, parking is available near the Giardini del Parterre. During peak season we recommend arriving a little early.
Yes, pets are welcome inside the festival area.

stay.title: Where to Stay
stay.subtitle: Apartments & rooms in Cortona
stay.intro: Stay in the heart of Tuscany. We have curated a selection of apartments, villas, and hotel rooms in Cortona and surroundings, across Il Casale and Hotel San Michele. Book directly and enjoy the magic of Cortona.
stay.ctaText: Contact us to collaborate with the Sagra della Bistecca and offer special conditions to our visitors.
